titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University Family Life Development Center records23-13-2854
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Eileen Keating

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-17 00:04:15 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Family Life Development Center records

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Family Life Development Center

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Iscol Family Program for Leadership Development in Public Service

did.physdesc: Manuals, videotapes, DVDs, CDs, booklets, manuscripts, dolls

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Historical information, guides and manuals, booklets, teaching material, curricula, and drafts of a text on family life; includes five anatomical dolls formerly used in child abuse cases, and articles representing opposing opinions on the use of dolls and material from the Child Protective Services Training Institute. Also, videtapes of Professor James Garbarino appearing on television discussing youth and guns.

Also seven DVDs and one leaflet relating to the conference: The Immigrant Child: Past, Present and Future which was put on by the Family Life Development Center in the College of Human Ecology in October of 2008. Joan Jacobs Brumberg was the project director.
Also 13 DVDs of various presentations, including the Iscol Family Program for Leadership Development in Public Service.
Includes records and 10 CDs concerning the Family Life Development Center Military Projects.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Articles and Papers Concerning FLDC Boxes: Box 2, folders 1, 5; Box 3, folder 1
Subseries A: Handbooks and ManualsBoxes: Box 1, folders 1-2, 5-6, 8, 26-27; Box 2, folder 2
Subseries B: Child Protective Services Training Institute (1971-2003)Boxes: Box 2, folder 6; Boxes 6-22
Series III. Reports/Proposals/Subject Files Boxes: Box 1, folders 4, 7; Box 2, folders 7-8; Box 3, folders 2-3
Series IV. Reviews Boxes: Box 1, folders 9-24; Box 3, folders 4-6
Series V. National Data Archive on Child Abuse and Neglect Box 25
Series VI. The Military Projects Box 26, Box 27
Subseries A: Army Family Advocacy Program Box 26
Series VII. Dolls Used in Counseling Sessions Boxes: 4-5
Series VIII. Conferences and Presentations Box: 23
Subseries A: Iscol Family Program for Leadership Development in Public Service

odd.head: General
odd.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/8130

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Family Life Development Center records, #23-13-2854. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Family Life Development Center (FLDC) was established by New York State in 1974. Its mission, to understand and act upon risk and protective factors in the lives of children, youth, families, and communities.

As a multidisciplinary unit of the College of Human Ecology, the Center accomplished its mission through research, training, and outreach. It served New York State, the nation and the international community with the areas of special interest including child maltreatment and family violence, youth development, children in residential care, HIV/AIDS prevention, and military family life.
In 2011 the Family Life Development Center merged with the Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center to form the Bronfenbrenner Center for Translational Research.

controlaccess.subject: Child abuse -- Study and teaching
Family life education -- New York State -- Ithaca.
